title: barbarea vulgaris f. perennis
intro: Barbarea vulgaris f. perennis, also known as wild rocket or land cress, is a perennial plant in the mustard family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an expectorant and to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's leaves and seeds are rich in vitamins A and C, potassium, and other essential minerals, providing various health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as supporting immune function. Wild rocket can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or added to salads, and its seeds can be pressed for oil, which is used in herbal medicine.
